description: Safety rules for genetic medicines - off-target prediction, genotoxicity, and toxicology markers.

facts.context: CRISPR off-target editing is predicted by:
facts.truth: sequence similarity to guide RNA, especially in PAM-proximal seed region (10-12nt)

facts.context: Unbiased off-target detection methods like GUIDE-seq and CIRCLE-seq work by:
facts.truth: capturing double-strand breaks genome-wide using tagged oligos or circularization

facts.context: High-fidelity Cas9 variants (eSpCas9, HiFi Cas9) reduce off-targets by:
facts.truth: weakening non-specific DNA contacts, requiring better guide complementarity

facts.context: Insertional mutagenesis from integrating vectors (lentivirus) poses risk of:
facts.truth: oncogene activation or tumor suppressor disruption at integration sites

facts.context: AAV vectors are considered non-integrating, but:
facts.truth: rare integration events occur, with enrichment at double-strand break sites
distractors: AAV never integrates under any condition

facts.context: CRISPR-induced double-strand breaks can activate p53, leading to:
facts.truth: cell cycle arrest and apoptosis, potentially selecting for p53-mutant cells

facts.context: Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) from gene therapies is characterized by:
facts.truth: elevated IL-6, TNF-alpha, IFN-gamma causing fever, hypotension, organ dysfunction

facts.context: AAV-related hepatotoxicity at high doses is monitored by:
facts.truth: elevated transaminases (ALT/AST) and may require corticosteroid management

facts.context: Thrombocytopenia (low platelets) with AAV gene therapy is associated with:
facts.truth: complement activation and thrombotic microangiopathy at high doses

facts.context: Dorsal root ganglion (DRG) toxicity with AAV has been observed:
facts.truth: in high-dose systemic or intrathecal delivery, causing sensory neuron loss
distractors: DRG is never affected by AAV
